analysis.reasoning: Core message: Lavrov repeats a quip that the US cannot have a color revolution because it lacks an American embassy—implying the US orchestrates color revolutions abroad—then adds a seemingly conciliatory note that they would not want the US to fall into deep crisis. Evaluation by narrative: RU1 (West immoral/hostile/decadent): Yes. The joke insinuates US interference in other states' internal affairs via its embassies, portraying the US as a hostile, subversive actor; the veneer of concern does not negate the accusatory thrust. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system): Not present; no reference to multipolarity, BRICS, or alternative architecture. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner): Weakly implied by the closing line expressing concern for the US, but it is not framed as Russia offering partnership or concrete benevolence; insufficient to count. RU4 (Russian history admirable): Not relevant. RU5 (Russia at forefront): Not relevant. RU6 (Russia culture/nature): Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes RU1.
